assumptions: each arm has a fixed Bernoulli mean and a per-trial prior score = mean + gaussian(0, prior_noise)
the initial visible pool is the top-n_visible arms by prior score; WIDEN reveals the next-highest-prior hidden arm
WIDEN costs a fixed integer price charged against the same budget that funds pulls; PULL costs 1; STOP costs 0
terminal decision is the posterior-mean-best arm among the arms made visible
common random numbers indexed by canonical arm and pull number; the trial world is shared across allocators
prohibited_inferences: transfer to adaptive neural MCTS progressive widening

scenarios.label: Clear best, noisy prior often hides it (12 arms, 4 visible)
Clear best with clean prior, almost always visible (widening should be wasted)
One better arm hidden among sixteen mediocre (2 visible)
Moderate prior noise, eight arms, four visible
Two near-tied best arms usually both visible (ranking-dominated)
